title: Port Bash external scanner and grammar
description:
Generate the Bash grammar package and port its external scanner to Go.

The Bash external scanner (~1050 lines C++) handles heredocs, variable expansions, test operators, extended globs, regex patterns, and string handling. This is a complex scanner that exercises most of the ExternalScanner interface.

Deliverables:
- Generate Bash grammar using tsgo-generate
- Port scanner.cc to Go ExternalScanner implementation
- Heredoc support: delimiter tracking, raw vs interpolated, indentation tolerance
- Variable expansion: simple variables, special parameters ($*, $@, $?), complex expansions
- Test operators within [[ ]] contexts
- Extended glob patterns (@(), *(), +())
- Regex patterns in [[ $var =~ pattern ]]

Tests:
- Parse real bash scripts (from popular repos)
- Differential test against C tree-sitter
- Exercise all external scanner token types
- Heredoc edge cases (nested, quoted delimiters, indented)

Reference: tree-sitter-bash src/scanner.cc
